brief.speaker: carol
brief.text:
Logs tell you what a service said. Spans tell you what it called — what it talked to, how long each call took, whether the downstream responded.

Different table. Same syntax. Fill in the source.

expected.sql: SELECT trace_id, operation, duration_ms, called_service FROM spans WHERE tags['service'] = 'chrono-portal-mirror' LIMIT 10

hints.text: Carol: 'Query error. The table with trace IDs and called_service is spans.'
Carol: 'Zero rows. The spans table has the data — try spans.'
Carol: 'Too many rows. LIMIT 10 is in the query.'
Carol: 'spans — the table that tracks inter-service calls.'

success.speaker: carol
success.text:
Different shape. You've got trace IDs, duration in milliseconds. And that last column — called_service.

It was talking to something.
